stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes for second night in a row after Trump declares ceasefire 'over'
stories.summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped 'dramatically' since the lastest attacks began.

stories.headline: International court tells BBC of breakthrough in Sudan war crimes probe
stories.summary: The ICC has been looking into atrocities committed in Darfur over the past three years.

stories.headline: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
stories.summary: The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

stories.headline: Photos: Israeli attacks kill nine people, including two children, in Gaza
stories.summary: Israeli air attacks and gunfire kill nine Palestinians in Gaza despite ceasefire, medics say.

stories.headline: US carries out second day of strikes against Iran
stories.summary: Trump claims Tehran ‘called’ to negotiate but casts doubt on prospects of deal

stories.headline: Graham Platner drops Senate bid after rape allegations

stories.headline: US Strikes Iran A Second Day; AI Rotation Trade on Watch | The Asia Trade 7/8/2026
stories.summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Paul Allen, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.

UPDATE: Bain says a special-purpose investment vehicle it set up for SK Hynix still holds a 14% stake in Kioxia. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil Fluctuates After Second Day of US Strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Oil swung as traders assessed the outlook for Middle Eastern crude supplies after US forces conducted a second day of strikes against Iran. Vessel-tracking data on Thursday showed a drop-off in transits through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Graham Platner Suspended His Senate Campaign. Here’s What’s Next for Democrats.
stories.summary: The deadline to pick a new nominee is July 27 and candidates are already lining up. State party leaders said they would hold some form of nominating convention.

stories.headline: US, Iran Trade Airstrikes as Fears Grow of a Return to War
stories.summary: The US military struck Iran for a second day and Tehran retaliated against American allies in the Persian Gulf, raising fears of a return to war after little progress in efforts to secure a diplomatic outcome.

stories.headline: US Senate nominee Platner halts campaign after assault allegations
stories.summary: A string of high-profile Democrats have withdrawn support for Platner following sexual assault allegation.

stories.headline: Platner Suspends Senate Bid in Maine After Rape Accusation
stories.summary: Mr. Platner’s withdrawal, which comes after a woman accused him of rape, creates deep uncertainty in a race that both parties see as crucial to their hopes of winning the Senate.

stories.headline: Democrats Grow Frustrated as Graham Platner Resists Dropping Out Quickly in Maine
stories.summary: Two days after a woman accused Graham Platner of rape, he had not given up his Senate nomination in Maine, even as state Democrats announced plans to hold a nominating convention if he steps down.

stories.headline: Australia, India strike deal on uranium exports during PM Modi’s visit
stories.summary: India has long eyed Australia's uranium to help meet a target of 100 gigawatts of nuclear energy capacity by 2047.

stories.headline: US, Iran launch more attacks as mediators urge warring sides to uphold MoU
stories.summary: Iran says it attacked sites linked to the US military in Kuwait, Bahrain and Qatar after US bombarded targets in Iran.

stories.headline: Flooding from Tropical Storm Maysak kills 39 in southern China
stories.summary: About 130,000 people have been evacuated from the Guangxi region while thousands of rescuers have been deployed.

stories.headline: Russia fuel crisis spills over to Central Asia as drone strikes bite
Russia bans diesel exports amid deepening fuel crisis
stories.summary: "This will enable us to increase supplies to the domestic market in July," Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak said.

stories.headline: Newsletter: Another day, another diatribe
stories.summary: In today's newsletter: Europeans leave the NATO summit with loaded guns plus strong statements from US President Donald Trump, EU ambassadors may go into overdrive over the 21st package of sanctions against Russia, and a European Parliament vote is set to rile up the political right.

stories.headline: Germany strikes deal to buy US Tomahawk missiles, Merz says
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz says he reached agreement with Washington during Nato summit in Ankara

stories.headline: Lenders Seek $700 Million After Japanese Firm’s Collapse
stories.summary: Creditors across Japan are trying to claw back more than $700 million from a payments firm suspected to have faked its financial statement for at least 20 years and abruptly collapsed into bankruptcy this week.

stories.headline: China's Tech Stack Outshines its Economy: 3-Minutes MLIV
stories.summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Market Warns UK’s Plan for Long-Dated Debt Needs a Rethink
stories.summary: Investors say that another bout of political uncertainty will force the UK to scale back planned sales of long-dated debt.

stories.headline: Second Person in a Week Killed by Federal Task Force in Memphis
stories.summary: Tennessee authorities are investigating both shootings involving agents working with the task force in Memphis.

stories.headline: Trump’s Mixed Messages at NATO Summit
stories.summary: As the NATO Summit in Ankara, Turkey, comes to an end, Tyler Pager, The Times’s White House correspondent, explains how Trump lashed out at European NATO allies while also declaring unity.

stories.headline: Security Precaution Led Trump to Use Old Air Force One in Leaving Turkey
stories.summary: The Secret Service is said to have asked that the president not use the Qatari-donated jet when he left Ankara. The swap deepens questions about the retrofitting of the new plane.

stories.headline: ‘Double cross, first class’: Why India’s Modi wins so many foreign awards
stories.summary: The prime minister was presented with an award during a visit to the Seychelles, adding to his large trophy collection.

stories.headline: IMF cuts 2026 world growth forecast, citing Iran war fallout
stories.summary: Global economy to grow 3 percent in 2026, as AI demand partly offsets energy shock from Iran war, IMF says.

stories.headline: Erste Shares to Rally 30% on Poland Growth, Goldman Sachs Says
stories.summary: Erste Group Bank AG shares are set to rally 30%, driven by growth from the lender’s expansion in Poland, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

stories.headline: Turkey’s Vestel Said to Start Work on Dollar Bond Restructuring
stories.summary: Turkey’s Vestel Elektronik is working with financial and legal advisers and is meeting bondholders to discuss a restructuring, while also renegotiating its loans with local banks.

stories.headline: Thailand and Malaysia Reach Deal to End Seafood Trade Spat
stories.summary: Thailand and Malaysia have resolved a seafood trade dispute, paving the way for fishery trade between the two countries to return to normal soon, their leaders said.

stories.headline: Strategic neutrality is Asia's new competitive advantage

Exclusive: Brussels puts trade ban with Israeli settlements on the table
stories.summary: The EU executive has laid out several options to further restrict EU imports of goods produced in Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territories, after a majority of EU foreign ministers called for clarifying what restrictive trade measures could be taken during a meeting in June.

stories.headline: World Cup sparks wave of racist misinformation online
stories.summary: False claims and AI-generated videos with racist overtones have circulated during the FIFA World Cup alongside genuine incidents of racism and xenophobia.

stories.headline: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
stories.summary: Graham Platner denied the allegation, calling it "categorically false" but said he was "taking the time to reflect on the best path forward".

stories.headline: U.S. Begins Second Day of Strikes Against Iran

stories.headline: U.S. and Iran launch fresh strikes following attacks on commercial ships
stories.summary: Iran said it had targeted more than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, after the U.S. launched strikes in response to attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran as Tehran warns of 'fearless' response
stories.summary: Speaking at a Nato summit, the president says the US will "probably" strike Iran again but added that "anything that happens will be over quickly".

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
stories.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

stories.headline: Russian fuel shortages bite – but will Putin change tack in Ukraine war?
stories.summary: Even in Moscow, authorities are unable to guarantee fuel supplies, but will economic pressure lead to talks or escalation?

SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: Iran nuclear and military damage revealed after restricted satellite images released
stories.summary: Targets hit include ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear sites, and naval bases.

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
